behavior_summary: ALTAR_EW::look responds to a player/actor 'U8P_ALTAR_EW::look' action by barking a short label ('Altar of Focus') to the viewing process — purely a UX/inspection response.

ALTAR_EW::gotHit handles physical hits on the altar: it validates the hit, checks that the target is the expected altar type and inspects its current visual/frame state, then produces sound/animation feedback, may create a focus item, and advances or consumes visual frames accordingly.

When a focus item is created, a worker process (ALTAR_EW::0270) is spawned to position the item, step its frame forward over time, play randomized SFX while it ages, and eventually either advance its frame to a completion state or destroy it.
A global flag hasOwnFoci is set when the altar grants/creates a focus for the player — used as a one-way marker that the player now 'has own foci' (inferred from a write).

Most behaviors rely on engine primitives (Item::create/pop/setFrame/destroy, Npc::doAnim/getDir, playSFX, urandom, spawn) to implement visuals, sound, and item lifecycle rather than complex game logic.
notable_control_flow: U8P_ALTAR_EW::ALTAR_EW::look: entry on Event 0 (U8P_ALTAR_EW::look) → call Item::bark(viewer_pid, 'Altar of Focus ') → suspend/return.

U8P_ALTAR_EW::ALTAR_EW::gotHit: entry on a hit event → guard on hit parameter validity → verify Item::getType and Item::getFrame to decide action path → playSFX and spawn a short FREE helper for immediate feedback → possibly call Npc::getDir / Npc::doAnim to animate attacker/owner → create a focus with Item::create and place it with Item::pop → write global hasOwnFoci = 1 when granting focus → optionally set altar/item frames or Item::destroy depending on stage.

U8P_ALTAR_EW::ALTAR_EW::0270 (worker): spawned by gotHit with created_item_handle → loop: position/pop item, suspend/delay, increment internal counter/frame variable, pick SFX via urandom and playSFX, setFrame on the created item or destroy it when thresholds exceeded → exit when lifecycle completes.

Decisions about creating vs advancing vs destroying items are based on Item::getFrame values and numeric thresholds (inferred constants), making frame checks the primary branching mechanism for progression.
open_questions: Exact numeric meanings and semantics of frame thresholds (e.g., the values compared to 0x0D, 0x18, 0x23) — these determine whether focus is created, advanced or destroyed and are inferred from tests.
Precise identity and gameplay effects of the created 'focus' item (type id used in Item::create) — the disassembly shows creation but not the gameplay properties of the item.
Exact interpretation of the hit_info parameter passed to ALTAR_EW::gotHit (is it attacker id, damage, or a hit type?) — inferred from usage but not explicit.
Whether the global flag hasOwnFoci is read elsewhere to gate other gameplay functionality (no reads observed in this script).
Purpose and internal behavior of the spawned FREE::1C19 helper process (spawned several times) — likely timing/visual cleanup but not fully identified.

main_function.name: U8P_ALTAR_EW::look
main_function.purpose: Produce a short inspect/UX response for the Altar of Focus by barking its name to the viewing process; does not mutate game state.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_ALTAR_EW::look) triggered when a player/actor examines the altar (viewer process pid supplied by engine).

function_map.name: U8P_ALTAR_EW::look
function_map.role: Handle 'U8P_ALTAR_EW::look' (inspect) event; display the altar name to the viewer.

function_map.name: U8P_ALTAR_EW::gotHit
function_map.role: Handle 'hit' events: validate input, play SFX/animations, create or advance focus items, and set progression flags.

function_map.name: U8P_ALTAR_EW::0270
function_map.role: Worker process spawned to manage the lifecycle, positioning, frame progression, randomized SFX, and eventual destruction/settling of a newly created focus item.

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn a worker to animate/manage the created focus item over time.

external_calls.purpose: Display the altar's label/description ('Altar of Focus') to the viewer process.
Retrieve NPC/actor facing/direction to orient animations triggered by the hit.
Verify the target object is the expected altar type before proceeding with hit logic.
Inspect current visual/state frame of the altar to branch behavior (create, advance, or destroy).
Play sound effects on hit, creation, or immediate feedback.
Spawn short-lived FREE::1C19 helpers for timing/visual cleanup after SFX calls.
Trigger attacker/owner animations to provide visual feedback for the hit or focus creation.
Create a new focus item instance when appropriate.
Place the created focus item into the world at offsets relative to the altar's coordinates.
Pick randomized variants for SFX during the focus item's progression.
Play randomized SFX as the worker advances the focus item's visual frames.
Advance the created focus item's frame/state during its lifecycle.
Destroy the created focus item when it reaches a terminal state or is consumed.
Read altar Z coordinate to compute pop placement for created items.
Read altar Y coordinate to compute pop placement for created items.
Read altar X coordinate to compute pop placement for created items.

writes.name: hasOwnFoci
writes.likely_effect: Marked true (1) when the altar grants/creates a focus for the player — appears to record that the player now 'has own foci' and is a one-way progression marker in this script.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: ALTAR_EW::look responds to a player/actor 'U8P_ALTAR_EW::look' action by barking a short label ('Altar of Focus') to the viewing process — purely a UX/inspection response.
